summary.intent: Address five Codex review findings on PR #727 (checkbox adapters) in the ars-ui repo, push a fix commit, resolve the review threads, and get CI fully green with a clean second Codex review.
summary.outcome: All five Codex findings were fixed and committed (2f042ba4 for the first round, then a second commit for the follow-up five findings). CI reached green across all jobs except Coverage which was still running at the end of the session. The second Codex review returned five new threads which were addressed locally and committed, but the session ended before pushing, replying, and retriggering.

repo: The repo uses `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity` as a parity gate that must pass before handoff.
Handoff lint gate is `cargo xclippy` (exits 0 required, warnings in touched surface are fixed before publishing).
Codex review is triggered by posting `@codex review` as a PR comment via `gh pr comment`.
Codex review state is tracked via GitHub reactions (`eyes` = reviewing, no unresolved threads ≠ approval).
xtask lint scanner must recursively scan directory-backed component modules and verify flat E2E fixture file contents mention the component.
The agnostic API is used to detect controlled checkbox state; native reset listeners should only call `on_checked_change` for controlled state.
Controlled E2E form checkbox fixtures must set `default_checked=Checked` to match their reset target state.
Dioxus Root must seed description/error presence from child compound parts before Control SSR attrs are computed.
Both adapters require regression coverage for direct compound usage without manual presence flags.
Adapter parity failures apply to the full implemented component set; delivery/presence checks stay scoped separately.

code.finding: Blur handler needed to clear pointer modality; controlled reset callback must only fire when checked state actually differs from default.
Mirror of Leptos: blur clears pointer modality and reset callback is gated on current-vs-default state change.
Visual assertions strengthened to assert positive numeric dimensions and state-specific deltas; match arms tightened and unnecessary `format!` removed to eliminate clippy warnings.
Scanner extended with recursive component-source scanning for directory-backed modules and fixture-content checks; shared 'mentions component' helper added for widgets and E2E.
Two regression unit tests added: one for directory-backed component child module scanning, one for flat E2E fixture files that exist but don't mention the component.

workflow: Codex review threads are resolved via GitHub GraphQL `resolveReviewThread` mutation using thread IDs.
Inline replies to Codex threads use `gh api -X POST repos/.../pulls/.../comments -F in_reply_to=<id>` with the fixing commit SHA.
CI polling pattern: 2-minute sleep loops checking `gh pr checks`, reactions, and unresolved threads in one compound command.
Multiple cargo test filters in a single command don't work for xtask — run separate invocations per test name.
Verification sequence before commit: `git diff --check` → `cargo xtask lint adapter-parity` → `cargo xclippy` → `cargo xfmt` → review full diff → stage specific files → commit.
Wasm compile verified with `cargo +nightly test --tests --target wasm32-unknown-unknown --no-run` (no execution needed).
Button Dioxus wasm coverage must be added when restoring a parity gate that checks the full implemented component set.

friction: Codex `eyes` reaction persisted for many polling cycles with no threads, making it impossible to distinguish an in-progress review from a stalled one.
Coverage CI job was consistently the last to finish, requiring many extra 2-minute poll cycles.
First `apply_patch` for the blur pointer reset hit the line without changing it, requiring a second explicit replacement patch.
Dioxus wasm compile was delayed by a build lock, blocking the validation batch.
Two clippy warnings surfaced in the E2E visual assertion code after `cargo xclippy` passed (exit 0 but with warnings), requiring an additional fix round.
Running two test filters in a single `cargo test -p xtask` invocation silently failed; required separate invocations per filter.
Session ended mid-loop after the second Codex review returned five new threads — fixes were committed locally but not yet pushed or replied to.
open_items: Second fix commit (addressing the five follow-up Codex findings) needs to be pushed to the PR branch.
Four new Codex review thread replies need to be posted with the second commit SHA.
New Codex review threads need to be resolved via GraphQL mutation.
A third `@codex review` trigger needs to be posted after resolving threads.
Coverage CI job result was not confirmed green at session end — needs verification.
Codex review approval state needs to be confirmed after the third review trigger.
